climate.description: Typical mid-June Alps: mild days, cool summits; prepare for rain and wind.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 70.

course_description.character: Brutal Alpine road sportive featuring iconic paved cols with relentless switchbacks, deep valleys, and high-altitude finishes in the French Oisans massif.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Alpe d'Huez ascent followed by the Vaujany wall, testing pacing and recovery on Tour de France-proven roads.
suffering_zones: Col de la Morte: 15.1km at 6.7% average with 10% max; long, grinding ascent after initial descent.
Col d’Ornon: Tame start at 3% ramps to 7%; positioned before the marquee Alpe d'Huez battle.
L’Alpe d’Huez: Legendary 12km with 8%+ average and 21 switchbacks; the heart of suffering.
Vaujany Final Climb: Deceptive 5km kicker to the finish with 14% pitches; punishes early aggression.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Abundant chalets and hotels in Vaujany and nearby Alpe d'Huez; GFNY offers camping and VIP options.
logistics.transport: GFNY-organized event with long competitive course (151km/4172m) and medium non-competitive (60km); starts/finishes in Vaujany with aid stations and perks.

history.origin_story: GFNY chapter in Vaujany leveraging Alpine icons like Alpe d'Huez; local benchmark evolving into international draw.

final_verdict.one_liner: Alpine road suffering at its finest: Alpe d'Huez glory awaits the paced climber.

citations.label: Cycling Competitions and Events | Vaujany and Alpe D'Huez
